prompt: A 19th-century naturalist scientific illustration in the style of Victorian expedition engravings, featuring a lone hiker descending a steep mountain valley. The landscape is rendered with fine ink linework and delicate watercolor washes, showing detailed alpine flora, rocky terrain, and distant snow-capped peaks. The composition follows a diagonal descent from top left (high altitude) to bottom right (lush valley floor). A subtle dotted trail marked by small red points traces the hiker’s step-by-step path downhill, becoming smoother as it approaches the valley. Include an inset frame in the top corner showing a close-up of the summit peak in the same engraving style. Muted earth tones, aged paper texture, precise botanical detailing, and naturalistic observation style. No modern elements, no photorealism, no digital artifacts.
tradition_approach: Used fine ink linework, watercolor washes, diagonal composition, and detailed botanical accuracy to mimic expedition engravings, mapping descent path as natural terrain traversal.
